source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Payments - Remote, US
source_jobs.description:
The role & it's impact:

As a payments expert, you will join Xero with a deep understanding of the payments landscape, navigating its complexities to drive commercial success. Your role is two-fold: leading the commercial relationships with large accounting and bookkeeping firms and championing Melio alongside our broader payment offerings to modernise how these organisations manage their cash flow.

You will act as a strategic consultant, managing the full sales cycle and helping enterprise firms move away from manual processes toward automated, high-efficiency cloud solutions. By navigating complex sales cycles and providing subject matter expertise on payment rails and digital automation, you will empower our prospects and partners to optimize their workflows and scale their operations effectively.


The team & how they connect:


You will join a collaborative enterprise team, working closely with Partner Success Managers and Consultants to design and execute growth strategies for our most significant partners. This role serves as the vital link between our payments technology and our largest accounting partners, ensuring a coordinated approach to landing and expanding the full platform.



Initially, you will focus on:

 - Driving sustainable adoption and revenue growth of the Melio payments hub across an allocated territory.

 - Analysing manual payment workflows to build compelling business cases for digital automation and integrated accounting.

 - Executing targeted outbound campaigns and office blitzes in collaboration with the wider enterprise sales team.

 - Utilising Salesforce and modern sales tools to maintain a predictable pipeline and accurate forecasting.
   
   

Where and how you can work:


This is a remote position based in the United States. We offer a flexible working environment that allows you to manage your territory effectively while staying connected with the team through virtual collaboration and occasional in-person events or workshops.



Here are some of the things we are looking for:

 - As a payments expert, you possess a deep, "in and out" understanding of the payments landscape, including payment rails and digital automation.

 - You bring a wealth of knowledge in B2B SaaS or Fintech, specifically within accounts payable and receivable environments.

 - A proven track record of exceeding targets in a partner or channel sales model is something you can demonstrate.

 - You possess a natural ability to consult with high-level stakeholders, from CFOs to practice leaders, telling a persuasive story about digital transformation.

 - Navigating complex sales cycles using established methodologies like MEDDIC or Challenger is second nature to you.

 - Your approach is highly collaborative, with an enthusiasm for working across teams to ensure partner success and long-term retention.
   
   

Compensation: At Xero, we value the impact and skills you bring to the team. We don’t just hire for a role; we invest in people. We believe in a Total Package philosophy - which means your value isn't just a single number on a paycheck. While base salary is a core component, we look at your compensation through a holistic lens that includes equity, performance incentives, and world-class benefits.

The expected base salary range for this role in:

 - California, New York and Washington: $120,000 - $150,000

 - All other US States: $116,200 - $145,200

Individual pay is determined by various factors, including geography, level of experience, and the specific skills you bring to the role.In addition to base pay, Xero’s roles are eligible for the following forms of variable compensation:

 - Variable Pay: Permanent employees are eligible to participate in our annual bonus and equity (RSU) programs. You may also be eligible for performance-based cash or equity (RSUs) incentives depending on your role level, and company performance.

 - Comprehensive Benefits: Medical, dental, vision, 401(k) match, and 21 days PTO,

You will play a vital part in building a stronger economy by empowering our community with the tools they need to thrive. In this high-change environment, your resilience and empathy will ensure every customer receives a best-in-class experience, turning complex queries into simple, effective outcomes.

You will join a collaborative Customer Experience team that works together to deliver high-quality support and drive positive customer outcomes like activation and retention. The team shares a growth mindset, constantly learning and refining their capabilities to stay ahead of our evolving strategy and technology.



Initially, you will focus on

 - Supporting customers through a mix of reactive support and proactive calls to encourage better product utilisation.

 - Embracing new tools and technology, including Generative AI, to enhance the delivery of our customer experience.

 - Using case management applications like Salesforce to accurately capture details and ensure seamless service.

 - Applying accounting fundamentals to resolve queries from both new users and seasoned professionals.



Where and how you can work

We are looking to add Customer Experience Specialists to our team who will work one of the following schedule:

Please note that the anticipated start date will be in 10 August 2026. There will also be an evening pay supplement for shifts ending at 8:00 PM.

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager (Front-End) - Small Biz Experience
source_jobs.description:
The role / impact

This is not a typical Engineering Manager (EM) role. As EM for the Small Business Experience (SBEX) team, you will be at the centre of a significant engineering challenge at Xero: improving the competitiveness of Xero's front end across the entire platform.



You will lead a horizontal engineering team whose internal customers are every development team at Xero. Your work will directly shape how fast Xero's engineers can build, how performant Xero's products feel to customers, and how effectively the organisation can ship features once across web and mobile. This is an opportunity to drive broad technical impact — not just within a single product or squad.



You will build and lead the team from the ground up, setting the technical direction, and embedding engineering practices that raise the bar for front-end development. Your front-end engineering background will be central to how you lead giving you the credibility to guide architectural decisions, mentor engineers through complex challenges, and shape a technical strategy the team can rally behind. Alongside that technical depth, your people leadership experience will be equally important: this team needs a leader who can hire well, develop talent, and build a high-trust culture from day one.



The team / how they connect

The SBEX team is a horizontal enablement team working across Xero to improve front-end competitiveness. Your internal customers are other engineering teams across Xero. Your external customers are the small business owners, accountants, and bookkeepers who rely on Xero to run their businesses. Getting the front end right — fast, consistent, observable, and maintainable is what makes both groups' lives better.



The team is currently focusing on

 - Contribute to and execute on a unified front-end strategy to address tech diversity across Xero's stack, moving toward a faster, more consistent platform

 - Improving page performance and latency in a systematic way, defining metrics, identifying bottlenecks, and driving sustainable improvements rather than one-off fixes

 - Building missing capabilities, to unlock new product experiences across Xero

 - Improving front-end observability so engineering teams have the visibility they need to operate and improve their products with confidence

 - Championing AI-assisted engineering workflows to reduce toil and increase velocity across the SDLC



Where and how you can work

This role can be based in Melbourne or Sydney, or remote along the Australian east coast. We support flexible working arrangements that balance team connection with the autonomy to work from where you are most effective.



Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - A strong front-end engineering background, whether built through previous hands-on experience or through leading front-end engineering teams closely, with enough technical depth to guide architectural decisions, earn credibility with your engineers, and shape a front-end strategy with confidence

 - Demonstrated people leadership experience, including a track record of hiring, coaching, and performance-managing engineers, and building high-trust, high-performing team cultures

 - Experience owning or leading cross-cutting front-end work such as shared libraries, design systems, platform tooling, or performance initiatives that had impact beyond a single team or product

 - A proven record of influence across teams and disciplines without direct authority, establishing standards, driving adoption, and improving the developer experience at a broader organisational level

 - Strong delivery management, with the ability to translate business goals into clear engineering plans and ship predictably in fast-paced, evolving environments

source_jobs.title: Accounting Professional? Discover a new career in SaaS Sales with Xero
source_jobs.description:
Are you a CPA or experienced accounting professional looking to leverage your expertise in a dynamic new way?



At Xero, we believe your deep understanding of accounting practices isn't just valuable – it's the key to our success in helping businesses thrive. We're seeking talented individuals with backgrounds in accounting, who understand the workings of accounting practices who are ready to transition their careers from traditional roles to become strategic advisors and relationship builders in the exciting world of cloud accounting technology.



This isn't a typical "sales" role in the traditional sense. Instead, it's about empowering accounting firms, guiding them through digital transformation, and collaborating to shape the future of their practices. If you're driven by impact, eager to share your knowledge, and looking for a career where your accounting acumen opens doors to new opportunities, we want to hear from you.



Why Xero? Why now?



The accounting landscape is rapidly evolving, and Xero is at the forefront of this digital revolution. We're passionate about making business life better for small businesses and their advisors as well as rewarding you in the process. We understand that your journey as an accountant has equipped you with unique insights into:



 - The daily operations and challenges of accounting practices.

 - The importance of accurate data and efficient processes.

 - The trusted relationships built with clients.

 - The potential for technology to transform workflows and client services.

 - We see these skills as foundational for a highly successful career with us. We're not looking for traditional sales experience; we're looking for your accounting expertise and your desire to make a difference.



What this unique career path offers you:



 - Impactful Advisory: You'll be a true partner, consulting with accounting firms to help them embrace cutting-edge digital strategies with the Xero platform.

 - Leverage Your Expertise: Apply your accounting knowledge and industry experience to solve real-world problems for accounting practices, guiding them towards greater efficiency and growth.

 - Relationship-Driven Role: Build deep, lasting relationships with key decision-makers in accounting firms, becoming their trusted advisor on digital strategy.

 - Autonomy & Growth: Work autonomously within your allocated territory, with significant scope for professional development and career progression within a rapidly growing global tech company.

 - Meaningful Contribution: Be at the forefront of a movement that is modernising the accounting industry and truly making a difference for millions of businesses.

 - Strong Earning Potential: We offer a highly competitive remuneration package, designed to reward your expertise, the value you bring, and your success in driving partner outcomes.



Who we're looking for:



 - You're an Accounting Professional: Ideally CPA-qualified, or with significant experience in an accounting practice, and a strong understanding of its operations.

 - You're a Natural Relationship Builder: You enjoy connecting with people and are skilled at building rapport and trust.

 - You're Solutions-Oriented: You're a problem-solver who can identify needs and offer practical solutions.

 - You're Curious about Tech: You have an interest in the emerging online accounting market and the potential of Fintech.

 - You're Ready for a New Challenge: You're looking for a change that leverages your existing skills in a new, exciting environment.



Is this the career change you've been looking for? If you're an accountant ready to translate your financial acumen into a dynamic advisory role at the intersection of accounting and technology, we encourage you to apply.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Technology Manager
source_jobs.description:
The role / impact

As a Marketing Technology Manager, you will step into a pivotal role as the internal Product Manager for our marketing technology stack, taking direct ownership of our core data pipelines, Salesforce ecosystem, and conversion event systems. This is a rare opportunity to directly guide a dedicated engineering team, empowering you to shape high-scale CRM and artificial intelligence roadmaps.

Your daily work focuses on configuring, delivering, and integrating premium SaaS applications to drive measurable return on investment and deliver hyper-personalised marketing at scale. By partnering with performance marketing, data engineering, and sales teams, you will make high-impact technical choices that improve audience targeting, enhance media performance visibility, and offer clear exposure to executive leadership.

The team / how they connect

The Marketing Technology team sits under Performance Marketing, operating as a high-velocity internal product and engineering function that owns our global tech stack. The team brings together artificial intelligence workflow development specialists and engineering pods who collaborate seamlessly across regions to build automated campaigns, lead-routing systems, and data-sharing pipelines. You will report directly to the VP of Marketing Technology, working alongside top-performing peers in an environment defined by accountability, shared learning, and a passion for engineering excellence.

The team is currently working on / Initially, you will focus on

 - Directing engineering workflows using comprehensive product requirement documents to build robust lead architecture and routing rules within Salesforce via LeanData.

 - Expanding our Segment customer data platform, utilizing personas to deliver complex user journeys, audiences, and an automated data layer connected to Clay, Unify, and Tray.

 - Launching flagship artificial intelligence initiatives, including our sales development representative automation project, by uniting data pipelines and large language model tooling.

 - Partnering with data analytics to instrument data-sharing pipelines like conversion APIs and identifier uploads to continuously improve bidding quality on third-party ad platforms.

Where and how you can work

We offer a flexible, collaborative work environment that prioritises digital connection and cross-functional alignment. Our team works effectively across global regions, utilising regular virtual synchronisations and team check-ins to stay aligned on global initiatives without any rigid office attendance mandates.

Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - Your background includes experience in marketing technology, sales operations, revenue operations, or product management, with a clear understanding of Salesforce and its integration capabilities.

 - You bring a proven ability to manage technical roadmaps, set feature priorities, and make high-ROI decisions that guide engineering teams toward impactful solutions.

 - Having a strong understanding of data flows, SaaS integrations, and modern tools like Segment, Braze, Google Ads, or DBT will ensure your success.

 - A genuine interest in exploring and applying artificial intelligence and large language model tools within a marketing or product context drives your work.

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills are part of your toolkit, making you comfortable presenting complex technical concepts to executive leaders.

 - You possess a natural curiosity for emerging technology, perhaps demonstrated through personal projects using Python or SQL, alongside a passion for solving complex problem sets laterally.

Apply even if your experience isn't a perfect match! At Xero, we hire based on your skills, passion, and the unique perspective you can bring to enhance our culture and team.

source_jobs.title: Head of Technical Innovation (APAC)
source_jobs.description:
The role / impact

Xero is at a pivotal moment in APAC, and this role sits right at the centre of it. As Head of Technical Innovation, you'll own the external product delivery space end to end — deeply understanding what accounting partners and small business customers need, shaping a clear product strategy, and driving execution with a network of development partners and cross-functional stakeholders across the region. Your work will directly shape what Xero's partners and customers experience, translating technical capability, AI advancements and real market insight into products and features that solve meaningful problems.

This is an individual contributor role with a leadership mindset at its core. You'll build compelling business cases, develop prototypes and proof-of-concepts, maintain Xero's competitive landscape across APAC markets, and ensure every launch lands with the internal enablement and documentation needed for success. From monitoring regulatory changes across APAC to influencing senior stakeholders, you'll be the connective tissue between market opportunity and product delivery.



The team / how they connect

You'll join the APAC Leadership Team, a tight-knit group of 10 spread across Singapore and New Zealand, who together run the APAC region. The team operates with a high degree of autonomy and collaboration, working closely with the Regional Director and a broad range of global functions to drive Xero's regional strategy. A culture crew keeps the energy up with always-on learning, development initiatives, and the occasional bit of fun along the way.



The team is currently working on

 - Building and refining the Practice Dream Suite roadmap for accounting partners, and the product suite for SME customers across APAC

 - Establishing development partner relationships and exploring grant and subsidy opportunities to grow the region's innovation capability

 - Mapping the competitive landscape across APAC's primary, secondary and tertiary markets to inform strategy and positioning

 - Laying the groundwork for an Innovation Hub that will expand into a dedicated, growing team over the next few years



Where and how you can work

This role is based in Singapore. While there are no formal rules around office attendance, the expectation is that you'll be a visible, active presence in the team — typically around two days per week, with Mondays and Wednesdays as the team's boost days. Beyond that, you have the flexibility to work in a way that suits both you and the demands of the role.



Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - You bring hands-on product experience and a strong technical foundation, including working knowledge of APIs and a practical understanding of AI and its applications

 - A track record of translating complex technical concepts into clear, compelling narratives for a range of stakeholders — from engineers to executives

 - Deep familiarity with accounting practice workflows and operations, or the ability to get across them quickly

 - Confidence working cross-functionally and influencing without authority, bringing people along rather than pushing them

 - An understanding of the APAC market and regulatory landscape, particularly as it relates to tax, compliance, and accounting

source_jobs.description:
The role & impact

As an Engineering Manager, you will drive the delivery of high-quality software by leading a high-performing inclusive team. You will act as the technical anchor, balancing predictable roadmap delivery with a relentless focus on team growth, technical excellence, and system health.



Your work requires a delicate balance between short-term product delivery velocity and long-term operational excellence. You will guide your team to hone their craft, champion AI-native workflows, and resolve complex technical challenges to deliver impactful outcomes that align with business and customer goals.



The team & how they connect

You will lead a results-driven team that operates with clear goals and ownership, fostering a high-trust environment where "blameless" problem-solving is the norm. The team works closely with Product and Design partners to shape solutions that truly matter to our customers.



The team is currently working on

 - Modernising architectures and improving CI/CD maturity to reduce operational risk.
   
   

 - Embedding AI-native engineering workflows across the SDLC to boost velocity and reduce toil.
   
   

 - Refining system design and architecture for high-scale, high-availability distributed systems.
   
   

 - Driving visibility into system health and technical debt to enable proactive improvements.
   
   

Where and how you can work

Xero is a Great Place to Work UK accredited company. We offer remote, hybrid or office based roles (hybrid and in-office in London, Milton Keynes or Manchester). Our offices offer boost days for connection and collaboration.



Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - Extensive professional software engineering experience, with a proven track record of technical maturity and delivering complex systems.
   
   

 - Demonstrated experience leading, coaching, and performance-managing engineers, including building and improving team capability over time.
   
   

 - Proven track record of translating business goals into executable engineering plans, balancing short-term delivery with long-term sustainability.
   
   

 - Hands-on experience writing, reviewing, and debugging production code, with the technical credibility to guide engineering decisions.
   
   

 - Experience building and supporting high-scale, high-availability distributed systems, including leading or overseeing incident response.
   
   

 - Experience leading teams in the adoption of AI-native engineering workflows to improve developer productivity and quality.
   
   
   
   

Apply even if your experience isn't a perfect match! At Xero, we hire based on your skills, passion, and the unique perspective you can bring to enhance our culture and team.

Join a Great Place to Work-Certified™ company where your voice matters.

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
OUR PURPOSE



Scheduling isn’t simply filling shifts. It’s finding the sweet spot that enables businesses to grow and team members to enjoy the perfect work/life balance.

At Planday from Xero, we aim to use Agentic AI to build a future where managers seamlessly can free up invaluable time for their business and teams. We’re not just building software; we’re on a mission to make shift work more human, to change work/life balance from a luxury to a reality for all shift workers. We’re using advanced technology to help humans reach their full potential. At work and in life.

Founded in 2004, Planday is headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark and helps create perfect schedules for hundreds of thousands of users across the world. Planday was acquired by Xero in 2021.




HOW YOU’LL MAKE AN IMPACT



As a Senior Account Executive, you will drive new business growth by owning and developing your territory. You will lead complex sales cycles end to end by deeply understanding mid market and Enterprise customer challenges and translating Planday’s capabilities into clear operational value for their business.

You will operate with a high degree of autonomy, taking ownership of pipeline creation, deal progression, and forecast accuracy, and bringing a strong point of view on how Planday creates value for customers.




WHAT YOU’LL DO



 - Own and grow your territory with accountability for new ARR

 - Build and progress pipeline through proactive prospecting and account planning

 - Lead full sales cycles with multiple stakeholders and longer decision processes

 - Run high-quality discovery to understand customer challenges and position value clearly

 - Lead customer discovery, deliver tailored product demos and lead commercial negotiation to provide solutions aligned to customer needs

 - Maintain strong pipeline hygiene and forecasting discipline

 - Collaborative, low-ego approach and ability to work across teams to win priority opportunities and improve execution




WHAT YOU’LL BRING WITH YOU



 - Proven experience as a Senior Account Executive selling B2B SaaS solutions

 - Track record of consistently closing new business through self-directed sales activity

 - Experience selling to mid-market and enterprise customers with complex needs

 - Strong commercial judgment and ability to manage your own territory as a business

 - Excellent discovery, communication, and negotiation skills

 - Comfortable running technical product demos and adapting them to customer use cases

 - Comfortable operating with autonomy, structure, and accountability

 - Curious, resilient, and motivated by building something rather than inheriting it

 - Experience selling into workforce-intensive industries such as healthcare, hospitality or retail is preferred.




SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE



 - Consistent delivery against new business targets

 - Healthy, self-generated pipeline with strong conversion

 - Accurate forecasting and disciplined deal management

 - Clear ownership of outcomes and continuous improvement in execution



This position description is intended merely as a guideline of the responsibilities involved in the position. The employee is expected to perform any other duties as reasonably required by their Manager.

At Planday, we offer you

 - Benefits like pension, health insurance, inclusive support for new parents and generous vacation

 - On top of your annual base salary, you are offered to be part of an Employee Share Plan

 - Growth and progression opportunities – we want you to grow with us

 - Flexible remote work

 - Strong social culture with lots of team and company activities

 - Meaningful work – everyone at Planday contributes to improving the lives of shift workers around the globe

 - Healthy work-life balance and autonomous approach to work. We trust in you and your abilities

source_jobs.title: Customer Experience Specialist
source_jobs.description:
The role & impact

At Xero, we are here to supercharge small businesses by making them more efficient and connecting them with big business technology. As a Customer Experience Specialist, you will play a vital role in this mission by acting as a brand ambassador and trusted advisor, ensuring our customers—from small business owners to seasoned accountants—unlock the full value of Xero. You will provide high-quality support and guidance through a variety of channels, helping users navigate their queries quickly and efficiently.

Your work is about more than just solving problems; it is about delivering a best-in-class experience through empathy and a passion for resolution. By proactively reaching out to customers and simplifying complex information, you will help build a stronger economy and empower the community behind these businesses.



The team & how they connect

The Customer Experience team works together to provide world-class support, aiming to become trusted and efficient business advisors to Xero customers. The team supports small business owners, bookkeepers, and accountants. They collaborate effectively across all teams at Xero, working to quickly and efficiently resolve customer queries through a variety of channels.



The team is currently working on

 - Engaging in proactive call campaigns to help customers achieve the best next action for their business.

 - Utilising modern tools and technology, including Generative AI, to enhance the delivery of customer support.

 - Managing and resolving customer cases using platforms like Salesforce to ensure accurate data capture.

 - Adapting to the evolving CX strategy and product landscape to support users in a high-change environment.



Where and how you can work

This team operates a hybrid working arrangement, requiring three days per week in the office, with Wednesday as a mandatory day. You will be able to work from home for the remaining two days. Please note, the initial training period for this role will require you to be fully in the office five days a week.



We are looking to add Customer Experience Specialists to join our team who will work one of the following schedules:

 - Sunday through Thursday, 11:00am - 8:00pm

 - Tuesday through Saturday, 11:00am - 8:00pm

Please note that the anticipated start date will be 14 SEP 2026.



Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - You bring experience from a service environment where you have successfully worked towards service levels and quality targets.

 - Your communication style is clear, concise, and authentic, whether you are writing an email or speaking on the phone.

 - You possess the confidence to make proactive outbound calls and the initiative to self-manage your daily tasks.

 - A growth mindset and a readiness to consistently learn and build knowledge are essential to your success.

 - You are a committed team player who proactively assists others and builds relationships through trust.

 - Proficiency with IT tools like Microsoft Office or Google Suite is required, and experience with accounting systems or case management tools is a bonus.



Apply even if your experience isn't a perfect match! At Xero, we hire based on your skills, passion, and the unique perspective you can bring to enhance our culture and team.

source_jobs.title: Senior Sales Operations Analyst
source_jobs.description:
The role / impact

As a Senior Sales Operations Analyst, you will serve as the analytical and operational backbone of the Canadian go-to-market function. You will play a vital role in enabling the team to perform at their best by synthesising data, insights, and operational rigour across the sales engine. This involves proactive forecasting of metrics, quota deployment aligned with business objectives, and maintaining internal systems at peak efficiency to drive meaningful growth for our small business community.

You will act as a strategic partner to sales leadership, translating complex commercial questions into clear, evidenced recommendations rather than just raw numbers. By owning data accuracy in Salesforce and designing sophisticated SQL models, you will ensure the business has a single, reliable version of the truth to inform critical decision-making and territory strategy.

The team / how they connect

You will join the Canada Sales Operations team, working closely with the Sales Operations Manager and the wider global GTM Operations group. This team acts as the central link between sales leadership, finance, and strategy, ensuring everyone has the visibility and tools needed to succeed in an autonomous yet highly collaborative environment.

The team is currently working on / Initially, you will focus on

 - Building and maintaining scalable SQL data models and Tableau dashboards to power regional reporting on partner performance and pipeline health.

 - Driving the adoption of modern GTM technology and AI tools, including Salesforce, Clari, and Gong, to maximise regional efficiency.

 - Designing and executing territory models and quota deployments that align with the current budget and business objectives.

 - Leading your contribution to the core sales rhythm through weekly forecasting, pipeline reviews, and quarterly business reviews.

Where and how you can work

Xero supports a hybrid working model that balances the flexibility of remote work with the value of in-person connection. Our Canadian offices provide a space for collaboration and community, and we encourage team members to join us for boost days and team activities to foster a strong culture.

Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - You bring extensive experience in sales or revenue operations, ideally within a SaaS or subscription-based business environment.

 - Your strong SQL skills allow you to build and maintain scalable data models that the business can trust and act upon.

 - Proficiency in Tableau or similar BI tools is something you possess, enabling you to create and document dashboards for non-technical stakeholders.

 - You have hands-on experience with Salesforce, including a deep understanding of data quality management and CRM administration.

 - A high degree of ownership defines your work style, as you naturally drive projects to completion and solve problems independently.

 - Communicating complex data insights clearly is a strength of yours, helping you build strong relationships with sales leadership and cross-functional partners.

Compensation & Total Rewards

At Xero, we value the impact and skills you bring to the team. We believe in a Total Package philosophy - ensuring your value is recognized through a holistic lens that includes base salary, performance incentives, and world-class benefits.

The expected base salary range for this role is $66,000 to $116,000 CAD

Individual pay is determined by various factors, including geography, level of experience, and the specific skills you bring to the role.

In addition to base pay, Xero’s roles are eligible for the following forms of variable compensation:

 - Variable Pay: Permanent employees are eligible to participate in our annual bonus and equity (RSU) programs. You may also be eligible for performance-based cash or equity (RSUs) incentives depending on your role level, and company performance.

 - Comprehensive Benefits: World-class health, wellness, and retirement

source_jobs.description:

As a Senior Solutions Consultant, you will step into a vital role as a technical expert and strategic advisor, leading our most complex and high-impact solution opportunities. You will act as a critical partner to our Sales team and customers, owning the technical solution strategy for strategic partners. source_jobs.title: Senior Engineer - AI Workflows
source_jobs.description:
The role / impact

As a Senior Engineer within the Internal AI Accelerator Squad, you will be at the forefront of Xero’s transformation into an AI-native company. You will act as a forward-deployed engineer, embedding yourself within various internal business teams to co-design and operationalise agentic AI workflows that remove manual toil and fundamentally reshape how our people work end-to-end.

Your impact extends beyond the code; you will lead the delivery of high-stakes AI agents from discovery to production, while acting as a technical partner and evangelist. By defining the patterns, guardrails, and tooling for intelligent automation, you will create the blueprint that enables the rest of Xero to harness the power of AI safely and effectively.

The team / how they connect

You will join a dedicated, high-velocity squad focused on boosting the adoption of agentic AI and intelligent automation across the global business. The team connects through a shared mission of rapid experimentation and "vibe-coding," working closely with product owners and SMEs to turn complex, messy workflows into elegant, automated solutions.

The team is currently working on / Initially, you will focus on

 - Designing and iterating on AI agents and workflow orchestrations using tools like Claude, Gemini, and MCP.

 - Integrating AI systems with core enterprise platforms including Salesforce, Workday, Slack, and NetSuite.

 - Developing reusable automation patterns and shared templates to be used by the business

 - Building evaluation and monitoring frameworks to measure performance and ensure the safety of AI-native workflows.

Where and how you can work

At Xero, we embrace a hybrid way of working that prioritises both flexibility and meaningful connection. You will have the autonomy to work from home, supported by regular boost days in our modern office spaces designed to foster collaboration, brainstorming, and team bonding.

Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - You bring a wealth of experience in building and operating applications or automations within production environments, particularly involving APIs and cloud infrastructure.

 - Your background includes hands-on delivery of AI-driven solutions such as agents, copilots, or RAG systems using LLMs like Claude or Gemini.

 - You possess proficiency in programming languages such as Python or TypeScript and feel comfortable navigating SDKs and webhooks.

 - A systems-thinking approach is part of your toolkit, allowing you to design agents with security, auditability, and controlled blast radius at the forefront.

 - You have a track record of partnering with non-technical stakeholders to translate business pain points into technical solution designs.

 - You are a natural coach who enjoys uplifting the AI fluency of those around you through pairing, workshops, and clear communication.

Apply even if your experience isn't a perfect match! At Xero, we hire based on your skills, passion, and the unique perspective you can bring to enhance our culture and team.

source_jobs.title: Engineer, Machine Learning Systems (AI Products)
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

You will lead the design and implementation of large-scale, production-grade distributed systems that power AI features for millions of daily users. You'll own the architecture decisions that keep our systems flexible, cost-effective, and robust, direct strategy for distributed systems, and manage technical debt across the AI Products estate.

Beyond architecture, your impact lies in lifting the technical capability of the entire AI Products team. You will champion engineering excellence, mentor junior engineers, and collaborate across Xero to enhance data usability - applying modern AI research, including Large Language Models, only once it can be engineered into reliable, production-grade systems.




THE TEAM

You will join the AI Products group, a diverse team of scientists, engineers, product managers, and analysts within our broader Data & Science division. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll partner closely with Applied Scientists to build the interfaces and harnesses that safely and reliably transition models from research into production. Together, this collaborative team reduces toil and delivers beautiful, data-driven insights for small businesses.




THE TEAM IS CURRENTLY WORKING ON:

 - Designing and building highly scalable, distributed production infrastructure to support generative AI features

 - Harnessing tools like Python, SQL, and distributed processing engines such as Spark or Dask to handle web-scale data workloads

 - Deploying to production environments running on AWS and Kubernetes Integrating modern Large Language Model technologies into product features once they're production-ready




WHERE AND HOW YOU CAN WORK

Xero offers a flexible hybrid working model designed to blend the collaboration of office life with the autonomy of remote work. You will have access to our modern office spaces, with expectations around office days and collaborative 'boost days' aligned to help your team connect and ship great code effectively.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years building and operating production Python (or equivalent language) services at scale - this is a software engineering role first; strong system design and coding proficiency are non-negotiable

 - A track record of owning services or pipelines in production, including operational/on-call responsibility, incident response, and managing technical debt over time

 - Deep understanding of distributed processing principles (Spark, Dask, or similar) alongside strong SQL capabilities

 - Demonstrated experience integrating ML models or LLM-based features into production systems - you don't need a research background, but you should be comfortable working alongside Applied Scientists to productionize their work

 - Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts for both business and technical audiences

 - A natural coaching mindset, with experience establishing engineering standards and mentoring other engineers

 - Familiarity with ML tooling such as MLFlow, TensorFlow, or PyTorch, and data orchestration tools like Airflow or Prefect, is valued - but production engineering depth matters more than research exposure

 - Nice to have: prior experience applying or fine-tuning LLMs in a product context, though this is not a substitute for the core software engineering bar above

Apply even if your experience isn't a perfect match! At Xero, we hire based on your skills, passion, and the unique perspective you can bring to enhance our culture and team

source_jobs.title: Business Development Manager
source_jobs.description:
The role & impact

As a Business Development Manager (the job title at Xero is Business Development Representative), you will play a vital role in generating demand for our platform and solutions within your market segment. Your focus will be on qualifying leads and generating prospects that translate into real revenue opportunities, acting as a key link to our Account Executive team.



You will establish yourself as a credible industry profile and a thought leader in your territory. By representing Xero at various events, you will attract high-value partners and help grow our market share, directly contributing to our mission of making small business life beautiful.



The team & how they connect

You will work as part of a coordinated team that values transparency and diverse perspectives to create a positive impact. This role involves close collaboration with the sales team to refine lead generation strategies and ensure a seamless handover of opportunities to Account Managers.



Initially, you will focus on

 - Executing outbound campaigns using phone, email, and social media to reach prospective accountants and bookkeepers.

 - Hosting and arranging Xero events for prospective partners throughout South Africa to build brand presence.

 - Researching potential partners and identifying key decision-makers to build a high-quality pipeline.

 - Utilising Salesforce to maintain accurate records and track the progress of all prospecting activities.
   
   

Where and how you can work

This is a hybrid role (3 days in the office) based in our Cape Town office.



Here are some of the things we are looking for

 - You bring a proven background in outbound sales and have a knack for generating business leads through multiple channels.

 - A natural communicator, you can build trusting relationships with stakeholders and articulate value with clarity.

 - You possess a strong work ethic and the ability to adapt quickly to new technology and software in a fast-paced environment.

 - Analytical skills are part of your toolkit, allowing you to assess performance and optimise your approach for maximum lead generation.

 - You are a team player who enjoys thinking outside the box to acquire new partners and solve problems creatively.

 - Detail-oriented and organised, you take pride in maintaining high-quality data and managing your territory effectively.
   
   

Apply even if your experience isn't a perfect match! At Xero, we hire based on your skills, passion, and the unique perspective you can bring to enhance our culture and team.

source_jobs.title: Principal Engineer (API Platform)
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE AND ITS IMPACT

This is a senior individual contributor and technical leadership role at the heart of one of Xero’s most strategically important initiatives.

As Principal Engineer for Platform API, you will define and drive the long-term technical vision for how Xero exposes its accounting capabilities to external partners (building on our platform) and to internal product teams who depend on a stable, well-designed API layer to move fast.



The decisions you make here will shape how millions of small businesses and their advisors experience Xero, not just through our own products, but through the ecosystem of applications and agents built around them.

Beyond the external surface, this role sits at the intersection of platform engineering and AI. The APIs you design and steward will serve as the trusted foundation for AI- and agent-driven automation across Xero’s accounting workflows; meaning you will be thinking not just about today’s developer experience, but about what it means for an LLM or autonomous agent to safely read, write, and orchestrate complex financial workflows. It is a rare opportunity to do genuinely consequential technical work at scale.




THE TEAM AND HOW THEY CONNECT

The Product Platform team is responsible for the core capabilities that other Xero teams build on; the APIs, SDKs, and platform services that connect Xero’s accounting engine to the world.

We work across domain and organisational boundaries, partnering closely with product management, AI/ML teams, developer relations, and engineering squads across the company to ensure our platform is coherent, reliable, and a genuine accelerator for everyone who builds on it.

As Principal Engineer, you will be a key voice in shaping technical direction not just within the team, but across Xero’s broader engineering community.




THE TEAM IS CURRENTLY WORKING ON

 - Our current technical stack includes an open source graph technologies powered by gRPC, and backend services built in modern languages like C#, Go, or Java.

 - We are standing up a developer platform to provide a seamless path from domain models to running APIs integrated with CI/CD and production runtimes.

 - You will help deliver across core accounting domains using the Supergraph model to consolidate our technical stack and developer experience.

 - The team is beginning a legacy burndown to replace or front expensive back-channel endpoints with coherent, strongly-typed interfaces and stable contracts.
   
   


WHERE AND HOW YOU CAN WORK

This role is based in our Vancouver office. We embrace a hybrid working model that balances the flexibility of remote work with purposeful in-person collaboration.




HERE ARE SOME OF THE THINGS WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - A deep background in backend or platform engineering at scale; ideally in multi-tenant SaaS or distributed systems environments, with strong proficiency in a modern language such as C#, Go, or Java

 - Proven experience designing and evolving external-facing APIs and SDKs, including lifecycle practices such as versioning, deprecation, and backward compatibility, with a genuine interest in developer experience

 - Familiarity with event-driven architecture (webhooks, message queues, pub/sub patterns, and idempotent consumers) and the ability to design for failure and recovery in these systems

 - A track record of leading complex, cross-team technical initiatives: shaping technical strategy, facilitating design reviews and RFCs, and aligning multiple stakeholders without losing momentum

 - Exposure to LLM- or ML-powered products, agentic architectures, or AI safety and governance concerns, and an understanding of how API and event design decisions affect model quality, safety, and reliability

 - A genuine approach to mentoring and growing engineering capability in others; from coaching individual engineers through to building communities of practice around platform and API thinking
